name: Aya Vision 32B
archDisplay: Aya Expanse 32B decoder + SigLIP2-patch14-384 vision encoder
note: Cohere's first vision-language model, pairing an Aya Expanse 32B backbone with a SigLIP2 encoder for multilingual multimodal tasks.

results.notes: Independent eval, text-only mode [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/press relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]

Text-only-mode SimpleQA score listed alongside the corpus's existing datalearner-sourced rows (which I re-verified and all still match exactly: MMLU 72.14, MMLU-Pro 47.16, GPQA 34.38, MATH 69.3, HumanEval 62.2, MBPP 70.43). No primary Cohere source (blog, HF model card, docs.cohere.com/docs/aya-vision, or the arXiv paper 2505.08751) publishes classic academic-benchmark numbers at all -- Cohere's own materials report only win-rate style metrics (AyaVisionBench, mWildVision). Marked independent per instructions since datalearner is a third-party aggregator whose original-measurement vs. vendor-relay status could not be confirmed.

Table 5 of the official Aya Vision technical report ('Aya Vision: Advancing the Frontier of Multilingual Multimodality', arXiv:2505.08751), multilingual text-only academic benchmark suite. The paper notes IFEval is English-only despite being included in the multilingual suite. Not previously tracked for this model.

Table 5 of the official Aya Vision technical report (arXiv:2505.08751), MGSM math reasoning averaged across 6 languages, part of the multilingual text-only academic benchmark suite. Not previously tracked for this model.

verificationNotes: Confirmed genuine via Cohere's official blog, TechCrunch (2025-03-04), and Cohere Labs' X announcement — release date corrected/confirmed to 2025-03-04 (a WebFetch misattribution briefly suggested Dec 2024 by confusing it with the separate Aya Expanse paper arXiv:2412.04261; independently verified this was wrong and 2025-03-04 is correct). HF model card confirms 32B decoder + SigLIP2 vision encoder, CC-BY-NC. All six benchmark rows (MMLU 72.14, MMLU-Pro 47.16, GPQA 34.38, MATH 69.3, HumanEval 62.2, MBPP 70.43) independently re-fetched from datalearner.com and confirmed to literally match. No changes needed.
